title: Revert " [InstCombine] Replace all dominated uses of condition with constants"

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define ptr @condition_used_in_gep_index_dominated(ptr %base, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp eq i1 %cond, true
  br i1 %cond, label %true_block, label %false_block

true_block:
  %idx = zext i1 %cmp to i64
  %gep = getelementptr i8, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  ret ptr %gep

false_block:
  ret ptr %base
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define ptr @condition_used_in_gep_index_dominated(ptr %base, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp eq i1 %cond, true
  br i1 %cond, label %true_block, label %false_block

true_block:
  %idx = zext i1 %cmp to i64
  %gep = getelementptr i8, ptr %base, i64 %idx
  ret ptr %gep

false_block:
  ret ptr %base
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests condition used indirectly through zext in getelementptr index calculation, involving type conversion and memory addressing not covered by direct condition comparison tests

define ptr @condition_used_in_gep_index_dominated(ptr %base, i1 %cond) {
entry:

  br i1 %cond, label %true_block, label %false_block

false_block:
  ret ptr %base

true_block:
  %idx = zext i1 %cmp to i64
  %gep = gep ptr %base, 1 x i64 %idx
  ret ptr %gep
}

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @select_dominated_by_branch(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %true_block, label %false_block

true_block:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %sel, %a
  ret i1 %cmp

false_block:
  %sel2 = select i1 %cond, i32 %b, i32 %a
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %sel2, %b
  ret i1 %cmp2
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @select_dominated_by_branch(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %true_block, label %false_block

true_block:
  %sel = select i1 true, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %sel, %a
  ret i1 %cmp

false_block:
  %sel2 = select i1 false, i32 %b, i32 %a
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %sel2, %b
  ret i1 %cmp2
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions phi nodes but not select instructions. The original dominance-based replacement would simplify select conditions in dominated blocks, but this pattern isn't explicitly covered in the summary's test coverage recommendations.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @nested_branch_same_condition(i1 %cond, i32 %x) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %outer_true, label %outer_false

outer_true:
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %cond, label %inner_true, label %inner_false

inner_true:
  ret i1 %cond

inner_false:
  ret i1 %cond

outer_false:
  %cmp2 = icmp ne i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %cond, label %inner_true2, label %inner_false2

inner_true2:
  ret i1 %cond

inner_false2:
  ret i1 %cond
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @nested_branch_same_condition(i1 %cond, i32 %x) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %outer_true, label %outer_false

outer_true:
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i32 %x, 0
  br i1 true, label %inner_true, label %inner_false

inner_true:
  ret i1 true

inner_false:
  ret i1 true

outer_false:
  %cmp2 = icmp ne i32 %x, 0
  br i1 false, label %inner_true2, label %inner_false2

inner_true2:
  ret i1 false

inner_false2:
  ret i1 false
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary discusses dominance but doesn't explicitly cover nested branch structures where the same condition is tested multiple times across different nesting levels. The original code would handle all dominated uses regardless of nesting depth.
